title: Thawing Frozen Vial of Mammalian Cells
steps: Warm media fo 37°C. Media may be DMEM or 1640-RPMI with 10% or 20% FBS.
Remove cell vial from liquid nitrogen and immediately put on ice to thaw.
Once cells are thawed, transfer cells to 15 mL conical vial and add 5 mL of media.
Centrifuge cells at 100 RCF for 5 minutes.
Aspirate media, minding to not disrupting the cell pellet.
Resuspend cell pellet in 5 mL of media.
Transfer suspended cells to T-25 and check for cell presence under a microscope.
Incubate cells at 37°C with 5% CO 2 for 2 days before passaging.
creator: Payam Amiri
